phone_1.text: {name}'s phone buzzes against the desk. 11:47 PM. Three unread from Ty.

phone_2.speaker: Ty (texts)
phone_2.text:
"yo {name_lower}. u up?"
"big tings tonight ahlie"
"pull up to the lot behind the building. 10 mins. dont be a wasteman"

choices.text: Leave him on read. Not tonight.

phone_yes.speaker: {name}
phone_yes.text: {name} pulls {his} hoodie off the chair. Ma's asleep. Baba's on nights at the warehouse until six. Ten minutes. Whatever Ty's got going in that lot, it starts tonight.

phone_no.speaker: {name}
phone_no.text: {He} flips the phone face-down. Ty's gonna chirp him about it tomorrow — 'man's got school in the morning' — like that's ever stopped anybody around here. Still. Not tonight.

window_1.text: Fourteen floors up. Jane and Finch spread out under the sodium lights — the towers across the lot, the plaza, the 36 hissing down toward Finch station. Somewhere past all of it, downtown glows like it's a different city. From up here you can see everything {name} wants and every way there is to lose it.

ma_1.text: Ma. Out cold on her side of the bed, the recitation still murmuring off her phone on the nightstand. Baba's side is untouched — he's at the warehouse till six, same as always. If {name} walks out tonight, this is who {he}'s walking out on.

ensuite_1.text: Their ensuite. Baba's razor lined up straight on the edge of the sink, Ma's kohl beside it. Twenty-two years of marriage in fifteen square feet.

washroom_1.text: The washroom. Somebody's towel is on the floor again. It's {name}'s. It's always {name}'s.

balcony_1.text: Cold night air, fourteen floors of it. Right below, the lot behind the building sits in its pool of orange light — that's where Ty wants to meet. Past it the towers, the plaza, and way south, downtown burning white against the sky. The CN Tower blinks slow and steady, like it's counting something down.

front_1.speaker: {name}
front_1.text: The front door. Eighteen years coming in and out of this door with groceries, school bags, excuses. Once {he} starts stepping out at midnight, that's a choice that doesn't un-choose itself.

front_2.text: {name} eases the door shut behind {him} and steps into the hallway. Fourteen floors down, then out into the cold — the lot, and whatever Ty's got waiting, are only a few minutes away now.
